title: salix × onychiophylla
intro: Salix × onychiophylla, also known as the willow-leafed saxaul or willow-leaved saxaul, is a hybrid plant of the Salix genus, which is a group of deciduous trees or shrubs commonly known as willows. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain due to its salicylic acid content, which is similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × onychiophylla include its potential to lower fever, soothe headaches, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Salix × onychiophylla can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote relaxation.
